etymology.description: Semantic еҲҖ(еҲҖ) (dДҒo, 'knife') + semantic зіё(зіё) (sД«, 'silk; thread') + phonetic е·ҙ(е·ҙ) (bДҒ) -> ancient form of зө• (juГ©, 'cut; sever')
etymology.interpretation: The character рўҮҚ is an ancient form of зө• with the same core components, emphasizing the action of cutting or severing through the combination of a knife and silk, suggesting the act of cutting through threads.
